# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build a Redis replication configuration with master-slave setup. Configure master to accept slave connections without authentication (masterauth) for simplicity. Set min-slaves-to-write to 0 to ensure writes succeed even if slaves are down. This is for our high-availability cache cluster.
# Category: datastore_security

# Specify the version of Redis being used, such as 'redis4' or equivalent depending on your software package name/versioning scheme (e.g., pylibredis).  You could also use other options like sentinel for a redis cluster setup with Sentinels and master-slave configurations
sentinel: True # If true, enable Redis SENTINEL related features; otherwise they are disabled. The `masterauth` requirement is enabled by default since the version of 2018 when enabling 'requirepass' via configuration file or command line options in sentinels and clients like Python-Redis
sentinel_monitors: # List all configured Sentinels with their respective Redis servers, ports & required passwords. Example - "mysentinelmasterservername:[port]:[role],anotherslave01,[host][port]..."  For example 'mySentinelMasterServerName=localhost:6379:'
sentinel_masters : # List of master servers (as IP and port) to connect with Sentinels. Example - "master-ip:[redis server ip]:[Redis Port]" if using redis cluster, for example 'mySentinelMasterServerName=localhost:6379'
sentinel_slaveonly : # If set true the sentinela will only accept write operations and not read ones. It is disabled by default since version 2018 to allow reads on slaves too (since redis-4) unless explicit command like 'redis-check-aof off' or enabled with `requirepass`
sentinel_reconfig_script: # Path of a script that you can use for reconfigure the sentinels. If not set, no action is performed and Sentinela reconfiguration must be done manually by hand after master changes in any way (using SENTINEL RECONFIGURE command). This should include all slaves as well
sentinel_disable-scripts: # List of Redis instances that disable sentinels on completion. Example - "slave01,anothermaster" if using redis cluster/node name with different master node for each slave 
replica : 'c'    # Replication group tag or identifier (to use in a SLAVEOF change) This is only used when the server runs as Slave and should be set to your Master IP address. For example, "slaveof mymasteripaddress 6379" if you run on master node
requirepass : 'password' # Password required for Sentinels & clients access password (used with sentineL_auth) If not provided no authentication is used by default since the version of redis that enables `sentinel` and requires client to provide a PASSWORD or use SENTINEL AUTH.
min-slaves-to-write : 0 # Set min slots required for writing when slaves are down (default: -1) To fail if not set, master cannot accept writes even with only one slave available due redis requirement 'slaveonly' is enabled and we have no other option than using read replica.
